heading: 9.7.14.5.12.Matrix Fragments formma.m16n8k128
content:
A warp executing `mma.m16n8k128` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k128`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.b1` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, with each register containing thirty two `.b1` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, …, a62, a63 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 97](#mma-168128-a).

  



[Figure: mma-168128-A]
MMA .m16n8k128 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .b1 type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k128
   Matrix: A (input matrix)
   Data type: .b1 (1-bit binary)
   Dimensions: M=16 rows, K=128 columns

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   Rows (R): 0 through 15 (matrix rows, labeled 0..15)
   Columns (C): 0 through 127 (matrix columns, divided into 4 groups)
     Column group 1: columns 0..31
     Column group 2: columns 32..63
     Column group 3: columns 64..95
     Column group 4: columns 96..127

3. THREAD AND REGISTER ELEMENT MAPPING

   The table uses notation Tx:{ay, az, .., aw} meaning thread Tx holds elements ay through aw in its registers.

   UPPER HALF OF MATRIX (rows 0..7), REGISTER SET a0..a31:

   Row 0:
     Columns 0..31:   T0:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     Columns 32..63:  T1:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     Columns 64..95:  T2:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     Columns 96..127: T3:{a0, a1, .., a31}

   Row 1:
     Columns 0..31:   T4:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     Columns 32..63:  T5:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     Columns 64..95:  T6:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     Columns 96..127: T7:{a0, a1, .., a31}

   Rows 2..6: Follow the same pattern (indicated by arrows showing continuation), with threads incrementing by 4 per row (T8-T11 for row 2, T12-T15 for row 3, T16-T19 for row 4, T20-T23 for row 5, T24-T27 for row 6), each holding {a0, a1, .., a31}.

   Row 7:
     Columns 0..31:   T28:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     Columns 32..63:  T29:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     Columns 64..95:  T30:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     Columns 96..127: T31:{a0, a1, .., a31}

   LOWER HALF OF MATRIX (rows 8..15), REGISTER SET a32..a63:

   Row 8:
     Columns 0..31:   T0:{a32, a33, .., a63}
     Columns 32..63:  T1:{a32, a33, .., a63}
     Columns 64..95:  T2:{a32, a33, .., a63}
     Columns 96..127: T3:{a32, a33, .., a63}

   Row 9:
     Columns 0..31:   T4:{a32, a33, .., a63}
     Columns 32..63:  T5:{a32, a33, .., a63}
     Columns 64..95:  T6:{a32, a33, .., a63}
     Columns 96..127: T7:{a32, a33, .., a63}

   Rows 10..14: Follow the same pattern (indicated by arrows), with threads incrementing by 4 per row, each holding {a32, a33, .., a63}.

   Row 15:
     Columns 0..31:   T28:{a32, a33, .., a63}
     Columns 32..63:  T29:{a32, a33, .., a63}
     Columns 64..95:  T30:{a32, a33, .., a63}
     Columns 96..127: T31:{a32, a33, .., a63}

4. DATA TYPE
   .b1 (1-bit binary values). Since K=128 and each thread holds 32 elements (a0..a31 or a32..a63), and 32 bits fit in one 32-bit register, each register holds 32 packed 1-bit values. Thus each thread holds 2 registers total (one for a0..a31, one for a32..a63).

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
   - Threads T0..T31 are each assigned to exactly one (row, column-group) cell.
   - Rows 0..7 use matrix rows 0..7; threads T0..T31 are distributed 4 threads per row (one per column group), cycling T0-T3 for row 0, T4-T7 for row 1, ..., T28-T31 for row 7.
   - Rows 8..15 reuse the same threads T0..T31 in the same pattern, but with the second register set (a32..a63).
   - Each thread covers all 32 columns within its assigned column group (32 columns wide per group).

6. PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS
   - Data type is .b1 (1-bit).
   - 32 consecutive 1-bit elements are packed into a single 32-bit register.
   - Each thread holds 2 registers: one register containing elements a0..a31 (32 bits = 32 x 1-bit values), and one register containing elements a32..a63 (32 bits = 32 x 1-bit values).
   - Total per thread: 64 bits (2 x 32-bit registers) covering 64 matrix elements across 128 columns (32 columns per column-group x 4 column-groups, but each thread is responsible for only one column-group of 32 columns, contributing 32 elements per register set).

   NOTE: The label at the bottom reads: %laneid:{fragments}, indicating the fragment assignment is indexed by lane ID within the warp.

MMA .m16n8k128 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .b1 type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k128
   Matrix: A (input matrix)
   Data type: .b1 (1-bit binary)
   Dimensions: M=16 rows, K=128 columns

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   Rows (R): 0 through 15 (matrix rows, labeled 0..15)
   Columns (C): 0 through 127 (matrix columns, divided into 4 groups)
     Column group 1: columns 0..31
     Column group 2: columns 32..63
     Column group 3: columns 64..95
     Column group 4: columns 96..127

3. THREAD AND REGISTER ELEMENT MAPPING

   The table uses notation Tx:{ay, az, .., aw} meaning thread Tx holds elements ay through aw in its registers.

   UPPER HALF OF MATRIX (rows 0..7), REGISTER SET a0..a31:

   Row 0:
     Columns 0..31:   T0:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     Columns 32..63:  T1:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     Columns 64..95:  T2:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     Columns 96..127: T3:{a0, a1, .., a31}

   Row 1:
     Columns 0..31:   T4:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     Columns 32..63:  T5:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     Columns 64..95:  T6:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     Columns 96..127: T7:{a0, a1, .., a31}

   Rows 2..6: Follow the same pattern (indicated by arrows showing continuation), with threads incrementing by 4 per row (T8-T11 for row 2, T12-T15 for row 3, T16-T19 for row 4, T20-T23 for row 5, T24-T27 for row 6), each holding {a0, a1, .., a31}.

   Row 7:
     Columns 0..31:   T28:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     Columns 32..63:  T29:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     Columns 64..95:  T30:{a0, a1, .., a31}
     Columns 96..127: T31:{a0, a1, .., a31}

   LOWER HALF OF MATRIX (rows 8..15), REGISTER SET a32..a63:

   Row 8:
     Columns 0..31:   T0:{a32, a33, .., a63}
     Columns 32..63:  T1:{a32, a33, .., a63}
     Columns 64..95:  T2:{a32, a33, .., a63}
     Columns 96..127: T3:{a32, a33, .., a63}

   Row 9:
     Columns 0..31:   T4:{a32, a33, .., a63}
     Columns 32..63:  T5:{a32, a33, .., a63}
     Columns 64..95:  T6:{a32, a33, .., a63}
     Columns 96..127: T7:{a32, a33, .., a63}

   Rows 10..14: Follow the same pattern (indicated by arrows), with threads incrementing by 4 per row, each holding {a32, a33, .., a63}.

   Row 15:
     Columns 0..31:   T28:{a32, a33, .., a63}
     Columns 32..63:  T29:{a32, a33, .., a63}
     Columns 64..95:  T30:{a32, a33, .., a63}
     Columns 96..127: T31:{a32, a33, .., a63}

4. DATA TYPE
   .b1 (1-bit binary values). Since K=128 and each thread holds 32 elements (a0..a31 or a32..a63), and 32 bits fit in one 32-bit register, each register holds 32 packed 1-bit values. Thus each thread holds 2 registers total (one for a0..a31, one for a32..a63).

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
   - Threads T0..T31 are each assigned to exactly one (row, column-group) cell.
   - Rows 0..7 use matrix rows 0..7; threads T0..T31 are distributed 4 threads per row (one per column group), cycling T0-T3 for row 0, T4-T7 for row 1, ..., T28-T31 for row 7.
   - Rows 8..15 reuse the same threads T0..T31 in the same pattern, but with the second register set (a32..a63).
   - Each thread covers all 32 columns within its assigned column group (32 columns wide per group).

6. PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS
   - Data type is .b1 (1-bit).
   - 32 consecutive 1-bit elements are packed into a single 32-bit register.
   - Each thread holds 2 registers: one register containing elements a0..a31 (32 bits = 32 x 1-bit values), and one register containing elements a32..a63 (32 bits = 32 x 1-bit values).
   - Total per thread: 64 bits (2 x 32-bit registers) covering 64 matrix elements across 128 columns (32 columns per column-group x 4 column-groups, but each thread is responsible for only one column-group of 32 columns, contributing 32 elements per register set).

   NOTE: The label at the bottom reads: %laneid:{fragments}, indicating the fragment assignment is indexed by lane ID within the warp.


  Figure 97 MMA .m16n8k128 fragment layout for matrix A with `.b1` type.

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =      groupID                              for ai where i < 32
            groupID + 8                           for ai where i >= 32

  col =  (threadID_in_group * 32) + (i & 0x1F)     for ai where i = {0, ...,63}
  ```
* Multiplicand B:

  | .btype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.b1` | A vector expression containing a single `.b32` register containing thirty two `.b1` elements from the matrix B. | b0, b1, … , b30, b31 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 98](#mma-168128-b).

  



[Figure: mma-168128-B]
MMA .m16n8k128 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .b1 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k128
   Matrix: B (input matrix)
   Data type: .b1 (1-bit binary / boolean)
   Matrix B logical dimensions: K x N = 128 rows x 8 columns

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table has:
   Rows (Row\Col header): groups of matrix rows labeled 0-31, 32-63, 64-95, 96-127
   Columns: 0, 1, 2, .., 7 (8 columns total, representing N=8 dimension)
   Each cell contains a thread identifier and its register fragment

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

   The 32 threads (T0 through T31) are arranged as follows:

   Row group 0-31 (K rows 0 through 31):
     Col 0: T0  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 1: T4  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (implied continuation)
     ..
     Col 7: T28 holds b0, b1, .., b31

   Row group 32-63 (K rows 32 through 63):
     Col 0: T1  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 1: T5  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (implied continuation)
     ..
     Col 7: T29 holds b0, b1, .., b31

   Row group 64-95 (K rows 64 through 95):
     Col 0: T2  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 1: T6  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (implied continuation)
     ..
     Col 7: T30 holds b0, b1, .., b31

   Row group 96-127 (K rows 96 through 127):
     Col 0: T3  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 1: T7  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (implied continuation)
     ..
     Col 7: T31 holds b0, b1, .., b31

4. REGISTER ELEMENT NAMING
   Each thread holds elements named b0 through b31 (32 elements per thread).
   These are labeled b0, b1, .., b31 within each thread's fragment vector.

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
   Threads are grouped by column (N dimension) in steps of 4:
     Column 0: T0, T1, T2, T3  (covering all 4 row groups = K rows 0-127)
     Column 1: T4, T5, T6, T7
     Column 2: T8, T9, T10, T11
     Column 3: T12, T13, T14, T15
     Column 4: T16, T17, T18, T19
     Column 5: T20, T21, T22, T23
     Column 6: T24, T25, T26, T27
     Column 7: T28, T29, T30, T31

   Within each column group, the 4 threads cover consecutive 32-row bands of K:
     Thread offset 0 (e.g., T0): K rows 0-31
     Thread offset 1 (e.g., T1): K rows 32-63
     Thread offset 2 (e.g., T2): K rows 64-95
     Thread offset 3 (e.g., T3): K rows 96-127

6. PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS
   Each thread holds 32 elements (b0 through b31).
   Since the data type is .b1 (1-bit), 32 bits pack into a single 32-bit register.
   Therefore each thread holds exactly 1 register (32-bit) containing 32 packed 1-bit values.
   Total bits per thread: 32 x 1-bit = 32 bits = 1 x 32-bit register.

7. ANNOTATION
   The figure includes a label at the bottom: %laneid:{fragment}
   indicating that the lane ID (thread index within the warp) determines which fragment is held.
   An arrow in column 2 points downward from row group 0-31 to row group 96-127, illustrating the direction of increasing row index within the K dimension.

MMA .m16n8k128 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .b1 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k128
   Matrix: B (input matrix)
   Data type: .b1 (1-bit binary / boolean)
   Matrix B logical dimensions: K x N = 128 rows x 8 columns

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table has:
   Rows (Row\Col header): groups of matrix rows labeled 0-31, 32-63, 64-95, 96-127
   Columns: 0, 1, 2, .., 7 (8 columns total, representing N=8 dimension)
   Each cell contains a thread identifier and its register fragment

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

   The 32 threads (T0 through T31) are arranged as follows:

   Row group 0-31 (K rows 0 through 31):
     Col 0: T0  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 1: T4  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (implied continuation)
     ..
     Col 7: T28 holds b0, b1, .., b31

   Row group 32-63 (K rows 32 through 63):
     Col 0: T1  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 1: T5  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (implied continuation)
     ..
     Col 7: T29 holds b0, b1, .., b31

   Row group 64-95 (K rows 64 through 95):
     Col 0: T2  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 1: T6  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (implied continuation)
     ..
     Col 7: T30 holds b0, b1, .., b31

   Row group 96-127 (K rows 96 through 127):
     Col 0: T3  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 1: T7  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (implied continuation)
     ..
     Col 7: T31 holds b0, b1, .., b31

4. REGISTER ELEMENT NAMING
   Each thread holds elements named b0 through b31 (32 elements per thread).
   These are labeled b0, b1, .., b31 within each thread's fragment vector.

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
   Threads are grouped by column (N dimension) in steps of 4:
     Column 0: T0, T1, T2, T3  (covering all 4 row groups = K rows 0-127)
     Column 1: T4, T5, T6, T7
     Column 2: T8, T9, T10, T11
     Column 3: T12, T13, T14, T15
     Column 4: T16, T17, T18, T19
     Column 5: T20, T21, T22, T23
     Column 6: T24, T25, T26, T27
     Column 7: T28, T29, T30, T31

   Within each column group, the 4 threads cover consecutive 32-row bands of K:
     Thread offset 0 (e.g., T0): K rows 0-31
     Thread offset 1 (e.g., T1): K rows 32-63
     Thread offset 2 (e.g., T2): K rows 64-95
     Thread offset 3 (e.g., T3): K rows 96-127

6. PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS
   Each thread holds 32 elements (b0 through b31).
   Since the data type is .b1 (1-bit), 32 bits pack into a single 32-bit register.
   Therefore each thread holds exactly 1 register (32-bit) containing 32 packed 1-bit values.
   Total bits per thread: 32 x 1-bit = 32 bits = 1 x 32-bit register.

7. ANNOTATION
   The figure includes a label at the bottom: %laneid:{fragment}
   indicating that the lane ID (thread index within the warp) determines which fragment is held.
   An arrow in column 2 points downward from row group 0-31 to row group 96-127, illustrating the direction of increasing row index within the K dimension.


  Figure 98 MMA .m16n8k128 fragment layout for matrix B with `.b1` type.

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =  (threadID_in_group * 32) + i         for bi where i = {0,...,31}
  col = groupID
  ```
* Accumulators (C or D):

  | .ctype / .dtype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s32` | A vector expression containing four `.s32` registers, containing four `.s32` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c2, c3 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 99](#mma-168128-c).

  



[Figure: mma-168128-C]
MMA .m16n8k128 Fragment Layout for Accumulator Matrix C/D with .s32 Type

OVERVIEW
Matrix dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=128
Matrix role: Accumulator (C/D)
Data type: .s32
Each .s32 element occupies one full 32-bit register (no packing; one element per register)
Each thread holds 4 registers total: c0, c1, c2, c3

GRID STRUCTURE
The table has rows labeled R (matrix rows 0-15) and columns labeled C (matrix columns 0-7).
Columns are grouped in pairs: {0,1}, {2,3}, {4,5}, {6,7}.

TABLE LAYOUT (R\C = matrix row \ column group)

R\C     Cols 0-1          Cols 2-3          Cols 4-5          Cols 6-7
0       T0:  {c0, c1}     T1:  {c0, c1}     T2:  {c0, c1}     T3:  {c0, c1}
1       T4:  {c0, c1}     T5:  {c0, c1}     T6:  {c0, c1}     T7:  {c0, c1}
2       [continuation arrow indicating pattern continues through rows 2-6]
...
7       T28: {c0, c1}     T29: {c0, c1}     T30: {c0, c1}     T31: {c0, c1}
8       T0:  {c2, c3}     T1:  {c2, c3}     T2:  {c2, c3}     T3:  {c2, c3}
9       T4:  {c2, c3}     T5:  {c2, c3}     T6:  {c2, c3}     T7:  {c2, c3}
10      [continuation arrow indicating pattern continues through rows 10-14]
...
15      T28: {c2, c3}     T29: {c2, c3}     T30: {c2, c3}     T31: {c2, c3}

DETAILED THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

Matrix rows 0-7 (upper half), registers c0 and c1:
  Row 0: T0 holds cols 0-1, T1 holds cols 2-3, T2 holds cols 4-5, T3 holds cols 6-7
  Row 1: T4 holds cols 0-1, T5 holds cols 2-3, T6 holds cols 4-5, T7 holds cols 6-7
  Row 2: T8 holds cols 0-1, T9 holds cols 2-3, T10 holds cols 4-5, T11 holds cols 6-7
  Row 3: T12 holds cols 0-1, T13 holds cols 2-3, T14 holds cols 4-5, T15 holds cols 6-7
  Row 4: T16 holds cols 0-1, T17 holds cols 2-3, T18 holds cols 4-5, T19 holds cols 6-7
  Row 5: T20 holds cols 0-1, T21 holds cols 2-3, T22 holds cols 4-5, T23 holds cols 6-7
  Row 6: T24 holds cols 0-1, T25 holds cols 2-3, T26 holds cols 4-5, T27 holds cols 6-7
  Row 7: T28 holds cols 0-1, T29 holds cols 2-3, T30 holds cols 4-5, T31 holds cols 6-7

Matrix rows 8-15 (lower half), registers c2 and c3:
  Row 8:  T0 holds cols 0-1, T1 holds cols 2-3, T2 holds cols 4-5, T3 holds cols 6-7
  Row 9:  T4 holds cols 0-1, T5 holds cols 2-3, T6 holds cols 4-5, T7 holds cols 6-7
  Row 10: T8 holds cols 0-1, T9 holds cols 2-3, T10 holds cols 4-5, T11 holds cols 6-7
  Row 11: T12 holds cols 0-1, T13 holds cols 2-3, T14 holds cols 4-5, T15 holds cols 6-7
  Row 12: T16 holds cols 0-1, T17 holds cols 2-3, T18 holds cols 4-5, T19 holds cols 6-7
  Row 13: T20 holds cols 0-1, T21 holds cols 2-3, T22 holds cols 4-5, T23 holds cols 6-7
  Row 14: T24 holds cols 0-1, T25 holds cols 2-3, T26 holds cols 4-5, T27 holds cols 6-7
  Row 15: T28 holds cols 0-1, T29 holds cols 2-3, T30 holds cols 4-5, T31 holds cols 6-7

REGISTER SEMANTICS PER THREAD
Each thread holds exactly 4 registers: c0, c1, c2, c3
  c0 = element at the lower column index of the thread's assigned column pair, upper 8 rows
  c1 = element at the higher column index of the thread's assigned column pair, upper 8 rows
  c2 = element at the lower column index of the thread's assigned column pair, lower 8 rows
  c3 = element at the higher column index of the thread's assigned column pair, lower 8 rows

GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
Threads T0-T3:   cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 0 and 8
Threads T4-T7:   cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 1 and 9
Threads T8-T11:  cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 2 and 10
Threads T12-T15: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 3 and 11
Threads T16-T19: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 4 and 12
Threads T20-T23: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 5 and 13
Threads T24-T27: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 6 and 14
Threads T28-T31: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 7 and 15

Each thread covers exactly 2 consecutive columns (a pair) and 2 rows (one from rows 0-7 via c0/c1, one from rows 8-15 via c2/c3), yielding 4 elements total stored in 4 separate 32-bit .s32 registers.

MMA .m16n8k128 Fragment Layout for Accumulator Matrix C/D with .s32 Type

OVERVIEW
Matrix dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=128
Matrix role: Accumulator (C/D)
Data type: .s32
Each .s32 element occupies one full 32-bit register (no packing; one element per register)
Each thread holds 4 registers total: c0, c1, c2, c3

GRID STRUCTURE
The table has rows labeled R (matrix rows 0-15) and columns labeled C (matrix columns 0-7).
Columns are grouped in pairs: {0,1}, {2,3}, {4,5}, {6,7}.

TABLE LAYOUT (R\C = matrix row \ column group)

R\C     Cols 0-1          Cols 2-3          Cols 4-5          Cols 6-7
0       T0:  {c0, c1}     T1:  {c0, c1}     T2:  {c0, c1}     T3:  {c0, c1}
1       T4:  {c0, c1}     T5:  {c0, c1}     T6:  {c0, c1}     T7:  {c0, c1}
2       [continuation arrow indicating pattern continues through rows 2-6]
...
7       T28: {c0, c1}     T29: {c0, c1}     T30: {c0, c1}     T31: {c0, c1}
8       T0:  {c2, c3}     T1:  {c2, c3}     T2:  {c2, c3}     T3:  {c2, c3}
9       T4:  {c2, c3}     T5:  {c2, c3}     T6:  {c2, c3}     T7:  {c2, c3}
10      [continuation arrow indicating pattern continues through rows 10-14]
...
15      T28: {c2, c3}     T29: {c2, c3}     T30: {c2, c3}     T31: {c2, c3}

DETAILED THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

Matrix rows 0-7 (upper half), registers c0 and c1:
  Row 0: T0 holds cols 0-1, T1 holds cols 2-3, T2 holds cols 4-5, T3 holds cols 6-7
  Row 1: T4 holds cols 0-1, T5 holds cols 2-3, T6 holds cols 4-5, T7 holds cols 6-7
  Row 2: T8 holds cols 0-1, T9 holds cols 2-3, T10 holds cols 4-5, T11 holds cols 6-7
  Row 3: T12 holds cols 0-1, T13 holds cols 2-3, T14 holds cols 4-5, T15 holds cols 6-7
  Row 4: T16 holds cols 0-1, T17 holds cols 2-3, T18 holds cols 4-5, T19 holds cols 6-7
  Row 5: T20 holds cols 0-1, T21 holds cols 2-3, T22 holds cols 4-5, T23 holds cols 6-7
  Row 6: T24 holds cols 0-1, T25 holds cols 2-3, T26 holds cols 4-5, T27 holds cols 6-7
  Row 7: T28 holds cols 0-1, T29 holds cols 2-3, T30 holds cols 4-5, T31 holds cols 6-7

Matrix rows 8-15 (lower half), registers c2 and c3:
  Row 8:  T0 holds cols 0-1, T1 holds cols 2-3, T2 holds cols 4-5, T3 holds cols 6-7
  Row 9:  T4 holds cols 0-1, T5 holds cols 2-3, T6 holds cols 4-5, T7 holds cols 6-7
  Row 10: T8 holds cols 0-1, T9 holds cols 2-3, T10 holds cols 4-5, T11 holds cols 6-7
  Row 11: T12 holds cols 0-1, T13 holds cols 2-3, T14 holds cols 4-5, T15 holds cols 6-7
  Row 12: T16 holds cols 0-1, T17 holds cols 2-3, T18 holds cols 4-5, T19 holds cols 6-7
  Row 13: T20 holds cols 0-1, T21 holds cols 2-3, T22 holds cols 4-5, T23 holds cols 6-7
  Row 14: T24 holds cols 0-1, T25 holds cols 2-3, T26 holds cols 4-5, T27 holds cols 6-7
  Row 15: T28 holds cols 0-1, T29 holds cols 2-3, T30 holds cols 4-5, T31 holds cols 6-7

REGISTER SEMANTICS PER THREAD
Each thread holds exactly 4 registers: c0, c1, c2, c3
  c0 = element at the lower column index of the thread's assigned column pair, upper 8 rows
  c1 = element at the higher column index of the thread's assigned column pair, upper 8 rows
  c2 = element at the lower column index of the thread's assigned column pair, lower 8 rows
  c3 = element at the higher column index of the thread's assigned column pair, lower 8 rows

GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
Threads T0-T3:   cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 0 and 8
Threads T4-T7:   cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 1 and 9
Threads T8-T11:  cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 2 and 10
Threads T12-T15: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 3 and 11
Threads T16-T19: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 4 and 12
Threads T20-T23: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 5 and 13
Threads T24-T27: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 6 and 14
Threads T28-T31: cover all 8 columns of matrix rows 7 and 15

Each thread covers exactly 2 consecutive columns (a pair) and 2 rows (one from rows 0-7 via c0/c1, one from rows 8-15 via c2/c3), yielding 4 elements total stored in 4 separate 32-bit .s32 registers.


  Figure 99 MMA .m16n8k128 fragment layout for accumulator matrix C/D with `.s32` type.

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =      groupID                           for ci where i <  2
            groupID + 8                        for ci where i >= 2

  col =  (threadID_in_group * 2) + (i & 0x1)    for ci where i = {0, 1, 2, 3}
  ```
